summary: Comprehensive testing of scanned PDF fix (pymupdf-based PDF-to-image rendering). All 6 backend tests passed. Frontend file upload flow verified working.
passed_tests: GET /api/debug/pipeline shows all stages ok including pdf_image_support with pymupdf version 1.27.2.2
POST /api/analyze/image with scanned PDF (/tmp/scanned_sides.pdf) succeeds via vision OCR path
Scanned PDF stages: file_received -> type_detection -> pdf_extract (0 chars) -> pdf_text_check (fail) -> pdf_to_images (ok) -> gpt_vision (ok) -> db_save (ok)
POST /api/analyze/image with text-based PDF uses fast path (pdf_extract -> gpt_analysis, no vision)
POST /api/analyze/image with JPEG still works (regression test passed)
POST /api/analyze/image with application/octet-stream MIME detects and processes correctly
POST /api/analyze/text with mode=quick still works (regression test passed)
Frontend: Page loads correctly with Actor's Companion title
Frontend: Paste script and Upload file tabs visible
Frontend: File upload tab shows drag-drop area with Quick/Deep mode options
Frontend: File selection shows image preview with Continue button
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scanned PDF fix fully tested. Key test files: /tmp/scanned_sides.pdf (2.7MB image-based PDF), /tmp/text_sides.pdf (text-based PDF), /tmp/test_sides.jpg (JPEG). The pdf_pages_to_images function in server.py (~line 477) renders PDF pages to JPEG using pymupdf at 200dpi. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_scanned_pdf_fix.py can be reused for regression testing. GPT calls take 15-30s so use --max-time 120 for curl tests.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - No bugs found. The scanned PDF fix is working correctly. The fallback path now properly uses pymupdf to render PDF pages to JPEG images when PyPDF2 text extraction returns < 10 chars, then sends the first page to GPT Vision for OCR analysis.
